summary: Emit deterministic INSERTs for traditions, texts, concepts, chunks (JOIN chunk_embeddings, section_path->array, embedding::vector), edges — every query ORDER BY primary key

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Corpus data integrity surfaces: chunk TOML 'tradition' fields are inconsistent — 'gnosticism', 'hermeticism', 'jewish_mysticism' are lowercase_snake while 'Neoplatonism', 'Jewish Mysticism', 'Greek Mystery' etc. are Title Case with spaces. There are BOTH 'jewish_mysticism' (1 text) and 'Jewish Mysticism' (5 texts) tradition labels in the corpus — true schism. Directory names are uniformly lowercase_snake (buddhism/, greek_mystery/, jewish_mysticism/). Traditions table has 11 canonical ids in lowercase_snake only. Export must normalize via directory basename to keep chunks.tradition / texts.tradition FK integrity with traditions.id; the chunk.id opaque string can stay as-is.

resolution.note: emit_inserts wired for all 5 tables. Live 2531-chunk export produces 10.05 MB gzip in 4.9s. Integrity: 11 traditions, 58 texts, 58 concepts, 2531 chunks, 2548 edges — 0 unknown text_id or tradition refs in chunks rows. Tradition normalization via directory basename documented inline; v1 corpus has real mixed-case inconsistencies that export.py hides from the Postgres contract.
